FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TeachingTip

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

WinUI TeachingTip (controls/dev/TeachingTip/TeachingTip.xaml + _themeresources + .cpp/.idl): an anchored (targeted) or window-relative (untargeted) non-modal callout that teaches/highlights a feature. The body is a SOLID surface — SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary background, SurfaceStrokeColorDefault 1px border, OverlayCornerRadius (8), an elevation shadow, and the 1px TeachingTipTopHighlightBrush top highlight (#0DFFFFFF dark / #99FFFFFF light, TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml:6/:28) — with (top→bottom) an optional hero image, an icon + title + subtitle row, the main content, and a footer button panel (Action + Close). A targeted tip grows a 16×8 tail/beak that points at the target; an untargeted tip has no tail. NOT light-dismiss by default (IsLightDismissEnabled=false): only the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close dismisses it.

The FULL 13-placement matrix (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates :115-264) is honored — Top/Bottom/Left/Right, the 8 corner variants, and Center — each mapping to the matching FlyoutPositioner placement with the tail pinned to the joined edge per the state's alignment setters (e.g. TopRight → tail at the LEFT of the bottom edge, :160-170). PlacementMargin nudges the tip away from the target (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 offset); ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (default true, the WinUI idl default) opts the popup out of the window bounds via the E4 windowed-popup seam when false.

1:1 tokens/sizes (TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml, unitless = epx): • MinWidth 320, MaxWidth 336, MinHeight 40, MaxHeight 520 (TeachingTipMin/MaxWidth/Height). • Content margin 12 on all sides (TeachingTipContentMargin); button panel top margin 0,12,0,0 (TeachingTipButtonPanelMargin); both-buttons split — Action 0,12,4,0 (Left), Close 4,12,0,0 (Right). • MainContent present-margin 0,12,0,0 (gap below the title block when content exists); icon→title gap 12 (TeachingTipIconPresenterMarginWithIcon); title-stack right margin 28 when the header (alternate) close button shows. • Title FontSize 16 SemiBold; Subtitle FontSize 14; body content FontSize 14 (Foreground = TextPrimary). • Alternate (header) close button: 40×40 (TeachingTipAlternateCloseButtonSize), E711 glyph at 16px, SubtleFill ramp, ControlCornerRadius. The footer ActionButton's DEFAULT style is the STANDARD button (TeachingTip.xaml:10 ActionButtonStyle = DefaultButtonStyle) — set ActionButtonIsAccent for the AccentButtonStyle the gallery samples use.

Open/close motion (host-driven, PopupChrome.TeachingTip): expand scale → 1 over 300ms cubic-bezier(0.1,0.9,0.2,1); contract scale → 20/Width over 200ms cubic-bezier(0.7,0,1,0.5) (TeachingTip.cpp:1660-1712; .h:234-235 + :304-307 control points) — both at full opacity (the storyboards carry no opacity keyframes).

public sealed class TeachingTip : Component

ActionButtonClick 

public Action? ActionButtonClick

Field Value

Action

ActionButtonContent

WinUI ActionButtonContent — the footer action button. Empty = collapsed.

public string ActionButtonContent

Field Value

string

ActionButtonIsAccent

The DEFAULT ActionButtonStyle is the standard button (TeachingTip.xaml:10); true opts into AccentButtonStyle (the common app override).

public bool ActionButtonIsAccent

Field Value

bool

Body

Body content (WinUI Content). When empty, the MainContentPresenter collapses (NoContent VSM).

public string Body

Field Value

string

CloseButtonClick 

public Action? CloseButtonClick

Field Value

Action

CloseButtonContent

WinUI CloseButtonContent — the standard footer close button. Empty = the close moves to the 40×40 header (alternate) close button (WinUI HeaderCloseButton VSM); set it to show a labelled footer close instead.

public string CloseButtonContent

Field Value

string

Closed 

public Action<TeachingTip.CloseReason>? Closed

Field Value

Action<TeachingTip.CloseReason>

Closing

WinUI Closing — fires before close with the reason; setting Cancel aborts the close (the synchronous slice of the deferral pipeline).

public Action<TeachingTip.ClosingEventArgs>? Closing

Field Value

Action<TeachingTip.ClosingEventArgs>

HasTarget

WinUI Target presence. When false the tip is untargeted (no beak unless TailVisibility forces one) and is placed against the WINDOW edge — WinUI PositionUntargetedPopup: default Bottom of the window, UntargetedEdgeMargin = 24px from the edges, PreferredPlacement picking the corner/edge (TeachingTip.cpp:571-668, :2074-2090).

public bool HasTarget

Field Value

bool

HeroContent

Arbitrary hero content slot (overrides HeroImage when set) — WinUI HeroContent is a UIElement, not just an image.

public Element? HeroContent

Field Value

Element

HeroContentPlacement 

public TeachingTip.HeroPlacement HeroContentPlacement

Field Value

TeachingTip.HeroPlacement

HeroImage

Hero image source (WinUI HeroContent): a full-bleed banner pinned to the top or bottom edge.

public string HeroImage

Field Value

string

IconGlyph

Glyph icon (Segoe Fluent Icons codepoint) shown left of the title (WinUI IconSource). Empty = none.

public string IconGlyph

Field Value

string

IsLightDismissEnabled

WinUI IsLightDismissEnabled (default false). When true, clicking outside dismisses the tip (and the surface swaps to the transient acrylic background — the LightDismiss VSM, TeachingTip.xaml:19-26).

public bool IsLightDismissEnabled

Field Value

bool

IsOpen

WinUI Boolean IsOpen (TeachingTip.idl, MUX_DEFAULT_VALUE false) as a CONTROLLED two-way signal: when set, writes from anywhere open/close the tip with the full expand/contract motion, and EVERY close path (close button, light dismiss, Escape, programmatic) writes false back — WinUI's OnCloseButtonClicked and its light-dismiss handler both end in IsOpen(false) (TeachingTip.cpp:1271-1276, :1382-1388). A cancelled Closing restores true (the WinUI deferral revert). Null = open via the trigger/OpenOnMount only.

public Signal<bool>? IsOpen

Field Value

Signal<bool>

OpenOnMount 

public bool OpenOnMount

Field Value

bool

Opened 

public Action? Opened

Field Value

Action

PartBubble

The solid callout card (WinUI ContentRootGrid): fill, border, corners, shadow, min/max sizes… The tail/beak and the 1px top highlight are composed AGAINST the card OUTSIDE this part (tail mechanics stay control-owned — restyling the card fill does not recolor the beak). Owned: Children (the hero · content · footer structure).

public const string PartBubble = "Bubble"

Field Value

string

PartCloseButton

Whichever close button is in use: the 40×40 header (alternate) close button when CloseButtonContent is empty, else the labelled footer close (WinUI AlternateCloseButton / CloseButton). Owned: OnClick (the Closing→Closed pipeline), Role.

public const string PartCloseButton = "CloseButton"

Field Value

string

PartHero

The full-bleed hero banner wrapper (WinUI HeroContentPresenter host). Owned: Children (the HeroContent/HeroImage slot).

public const string PartHero = "Hero"

Field Value

string

PartTitle

The title text — a TextEl part: customize via Parts.Set<TextEl>(TeachingTip.PartTitle, t => t with { … }). Owned: none (pure styling).

public const string PartTitle = "Title"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract.

public TemplateParts? Parts

Field Value

TemplateParts

PlacementMargin

WinUI PlacementMargin (idl:119): extra distance between the target and the tip, applied on the placement axis (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 read it as the offset).

public float PlacementMargin

Field Value

float

PreferredPlacement

WinUI PreferredPlacement (default Auto = Top for a targeted tip).

public TeachingTip.PlacementMode PreferredPlacement

Field Value

TeachingTip.PlacementMode

ShouldConstrainToRootBounds

WinUI ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (default TRUE — the tip stays inside the window). False → the popup goes windowed via the E4 seam and may escape the window.

public bool ShouldConstrainToRootBounds

Field Value

bool

Subtitle 

public string Subtitle

Field Value

string

TailVisibility 

public TeachingTip.TailVisibilityMode TailVisibility

Field Value

TeachingTip.TailVisibilityMode

Target

WinUI FrameworkElement Target (TeachingTip.idl) as the engine's anchor-thunk seam: the node the targeted tip points at (capture it via OnRealized on the target element). Null with HasTarget=true anchors to this component's own wrapper/trigger (the pre-Target compat behavior); HasTarget=false ignores it (untargeted window-edge placement).

public Func<NodeHandle>? Target

Field Value

Func<NodeHandle>

Title 

public string Title

Field Value

string

TriggerLabel

Optional convenience trigger button label — WinUI's TeachingTip has NO trigger UI of its own (it is opened via IsOpen only, TeachingTip.idl); empty (the default) renders no trigger. Set it for the gallery-style "button that shows the tip" composition (the wrapper then doubles as the anchor).

public string TriggerLabel

Field Value

string

Methods

Create(string, string, string)

Embed a TeachingTip with a trigger label, title, and body (kept call-site-compatible with the prior API).

public static Element Create(string triggerLabel, string title, string body)

triggerLabel string
title string
body string

Element

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
